compositional_truth_check.new_introductions: None identified; debris consistent with previous states.

vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry identifies P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) as shared in a black pot. Visual evidence shows only one primary specimen (P4) in the lower black pot. P2 is considered a systemic loss due to lack of vegetative presence.

plant_audit.p3_pothos: Stressed; leaf chlorosis and necrosis observed on the trailing specimen. Rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.p4_silver_guest: Single leaf structure remains, showing signs of potential dehydration or lack of vigor.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark; no visible surface cracking.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Debris (white perlite/egg fragments) remains static across the sequence.

temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Minor postural shift in P3 leaf.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Significant lighting drop; specimen visibility obscured.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Return to baseline lighting; P3 leaf necrosis has progressed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of decline. P3 exhibits clear signs of nutrient deficiency or root stress (chlorosis). P2 is effectively lost. P1 and P4 are in stasis, showing no active growth.
vision_report.anomalies: The 'Silver Guest' (P4) appears as a single, isolated leaf structure, suggesting the loss of its companion (P2) or a failure to thrive.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a struggling indoor environment. The Pothos (P3) is the most symptomatic, showing progressive leaf decay. The Mexican Mint (P2) is absent, indicating a systemic loss. The overall biome lacks the vitality expected of a healthy indoor setup, with static growth and signs of physiological stress across all specimens.
